prior_session_note: S114 shipped the G4 in-world leader crown LIVE (commit c2c1e80, deploy 501885e); render-only, PROTOCOL_VERSION held 14. Tier-1 G3+G4 reconciled COMPLETE. Remaining Tier-1 (G1b MOTION, G2 family traits) is owner-design-gated. S115 advances the next NON-gated work while the owner has not yet playtested.
council_result: 2-way (GROK-ANALYST grok-4.20-reasoning + Claude Supervisor; Gemini 429 credits-depleted, S112-S114 precedent). Grok verdict REJECT-AND-SPLIT. Supervisor PRIME-AUDIT synthesis: P1 ADOPT-WITH-FIX (keep Vortex pre-substep slot — post-solver damp would damp the constraint's corrective velocity = MORE dead; per-prim damp is order-independent; fouled-skip in-pass). P2 REDESIGN (Grok's escape-velocity REJECT is valid against a CONSTANT impulse -> overruled to ADOPT-WITH-FIX by bounding the tangential SPEED, non-accumulating by construction; low/smooth speed for 10Hz readability). P3 ADOPT-WITH-FIX (HELLO pubkey purely additive, no D1 consumer requires it, test absence; warrant reuses the injective domain-separated buildAttestPayload encoding; WebCrypto ECDSA = raw non-malleable sigs). OVERRULED the full SPLIT: the 3 priorities are independent (P1/P2 state+physics+combos; P3 net) with per-priority commit+CHECK+tests = equivalent review depth; user explicitly requested a full batch + pre-approved autonomous run. Bundle headroom ~140KiB (Grok 'tight' overstated). PDR: .claude/plans/2026-06-29_PDR_S115_Combo_Behaviors_HostMigD1.md

priorities.label: S115 BATCH (one approval, 3 sub-priorities executed sequentially P1->P2->P3): P1 ANCHOR anti-drift (Dot->Square magic combo gets a host-only Verlet drift-damp so anchored structures resist being shoved; new anchorStabilize.ts + isAnchorCombo + ANCHOR_DRIFT_DAMP; render/sim-only, no wire bump). P2 SPINDLE swirl (Line->Circle magic combo imparts a BOUNDED tangential impulse to free sparks so they orbit; 90deg-rotated Vortex clone with a tangential-SPEED cap = non-accumulating; new spindle.ts + isSpindleCombo + SPINDLE_* constants; no wire bump). P3 HOST-MIGRATION D1 (identity plumbing, FEATURE-FLAGGED OFF, no protocol bump): client ephemeral ECDSA identity at boot + joiner pubkey additive-optional in HELLO + pure SuccessionWarrant build/sign/verify helpers + unit tests; no MIGRATION_CLAIM/detection/takeover (D2-D4). PROTOCOL_VERSION held 14 across the whole batch. Standard tier (highest-of-batch; D1 driver).
priorities.check_method: All 3 sub-priorities shipped as 3 revertible commits, each gated GREEN, then DEPLOYED LIVE. P1 ANCHOR planted-joint (04ced0c): Dot<->Square magic combo floors its territorial stiffnessMultiplier (ANCHOR_STIFFNESS_FLOOR 0.7) so anchored structures resist the S49 enemy-territory sag. A.0-during-execution found the PDR's 'damp prim drift velocity' was a NO-OP (placed prims are never Verlet-integrated — verletStepAll steps only freeSparks, collisions spark-only, placement deletes the spark, hazards erase/sever not shove); reframed to the territorial-stiffness floor (same owner-approved verb, engine-correct mechanism). new state/anchorStabilize.ts (host-only, per-bond idempotent => order-independent, fouled/deleted skip) + isAnchorCombo + wired in physicsLoop after computeTerritorialInfluence. Caught+reconciled the STALE S91 'forward-key only' comment (S98 made Anchor/Spindle order-SYMMETRIC; test caught isAnchorCombo(Square,Dot)===true). P2 SPINDLE bounded-swirl (e80854f): Line<->Circle magic combo imparts a TANGENTIAL impulse to nearby free sparks (orbit, vs Vortex radial suck-in). Council (Grok) REJECTed the naive constant-impulse design for escape-velocity accumulation; FIXED by bounding the tangential SPEED (project velocity onto swirl axis, add only up to SPINDLE_MAX_TANGENTIAL_SPEED headroom) => non-accumulating by construction, proven by a 500-tick unit test. new state/spindle.ts (90deg-rotated applyVortexPull clone; bond-id canonical sort; deterministic vector math, no sin/cos) + isSpindleCombo + SPINDLE_* constants + wired in physicsLoop. P3 host-migration D1 (707c973, DORMANT/feature-flagged off): net/successionWarrant.ts (buildWarrantPayload injective length-prefixed+domain-separated encoding, signWarrant, verifyWarrant chaining to the room-code commitment via host-pubkey fingerprint + fail-closed, warrantedPubkeyForSeat) + generateClientIdentity + HostIdentity.sign() (additive) + ADDITIVE-OPTIONAL HELLO clientPubkeyB64 (fail-closed parse, unpopulated by live sends). No MIGRATION_CLAIM/detection/takeover/boot-wiring (carried D2-D4). P1+P2 render/sim-only, P3 additive => PROTOCOL_VERSION held 14 across the whole batch; save.replay byte-identical (24/24). GATES: tsc 0; vitest 1735->1779 (+44: 9 anchor + 12 spindle + 14 D1 + the +9 net from prior count reconcile... actual final 1779/1779); build entry 611.0/750 KiB (+1.4 over the session). DEPLOYED via npm run deploy -> gh-pages classic Pages: spark-online.space serves this build (deploy script self-verified live hash assets/index-BZSCuCtI.js == built). IN-BROWSER smoke: new modules load + run with 0 console errors; the live-orbit drive was blocked by a hand-hacked-PLAYING-world NaN ARTIFACT (proven via a no-Spindle control that ALSO NaNs — not a code defect; stepPhysics wiring is covered by physicsLoop.test.ts + save.replay full-tick determinism). RALPH:PATROL PASS on all 3. Council 2-way (Grok-4.20 ANALYST + Opus Supervisor; Gemini 429) + PRIME-AUDIT; Standard tier.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: S115-P1 #scope-the-mechanism-against-the-engine-not-the-pdrs-mental-model: the PDR's 'damp the prim drift velocity' Anchor design was a NO-OP against the real engine — placed primitives are NEVER Verlet-integrated (verletStepAll steps only world.freeSparks; resolveCollisions is spark-only; placement DELETEs the spark; hazards erase/sever a structure, never shove it) so there is no free momentum to damp. A.0-during-execution (read verlet.ts + physicsLoop + how potato/territory actually move a structure) revealed the ONLY real structural instability is the S49 territorial stiffnessMultiplier sag; reframed Anchor to FLOOR that sag (same owner-approved 'planted joint' verb, engine-correct mechanism). LESSON: verify the PDR's IMPLEMENTATION mechanism against the integrator BEFORE coding, not just the feature's intent — the verb survived, the mechanism didn't.

S115-P1 #a-stale-source-comment-is-a-claim-grep-the-construction-not-the-comment: the S91 'forward-key only — Square->Dot stays a placeholder' combos comment was SUPERSEDED by the S98 order-symmetry mirror loop (which mirrors every non-self magic pair, Anchor+Spindle included); my new isAnchorCombo docstring AND a unit test repeated the stale claim until the test caught it (isAnchorCombo(Square,Dot)===true). LESSON: trust the table-CONSTRUCTION code over an adjacent prose comment; reconcile the stale comment as part of the work; the symmetric reverse earns the BEHAVIOR, not just the income (Vortex S98 precedent).

S115-P2 #bound-the-accumulated-STATE-not-the-per-tick-DELTA: Grok's REJECT of the naive Spindle (constant tangential impulse -> escape velocity) was a real kill-shot; capping the per-tick IMPULSE does NOT fix it (it still accumulates across ticks since the spark stays in range). The fix bounds the RESULT: project the spark's current velocity onto the swirl axis and only add up to the headroom to SPINDLE_MAX_TANGENTIAL_SPEED. Provably non-accumulating; a 500-tick unit test asserts the bound holds every tick. LESSON: when a behavior adds to a quantity each tick, bound the STATE (resulting speed), not the DELTA (per-tick impulse).

S115-P2 #a-hand-hacked-PLAYING-world-cannot-runtime-verify-a-behavior: driving the hidden preview by setting world.gameState='PLAYING' on a TITLE world (a) ran tick++ but SKIPPED stepPhysics until I cleared the forceNonet sudoku-FREEZE branch, then (b) NaN'd within ~14 ticks — a no-Spindle CONTROL spark ALSO NaN'd, proving the uninitialized-match state (territory/scoring/collision on a title world) is the NaN source, NOT the new code. LESSON: the app gates stepPhysics on its SCREEN state, not world.gameState; a hand-forced PLAYING world is not a valid runtime fixture. Use physicsLoop.test.ts (real stepPhysics) for wiring + the browser load-smoke for 'no console error'; to truly drive live, start a real match via the title buttons (S113 method).

S115-P3 #ship-the-PRIMITIVES-dormant-defer-the-LIVE-WIRING: host-migration D1 is the textbook 'land the tested foundation, activate later' phase — the warrant build/sign/verify + client identity + the additive-optional HELLO pubkey field all ship now (14 tests, real WebCrypto), but NOTHING is wired into a live decision path (no boot-time keygen, no live HELLO populates the field, no MIGRATION_CLAIM). This keeps a security-sensitive netcode feature at ZERO behavior-change before the owner playtests, while D2-D4 build on a proven base. KEY trust property: verifyWarrant chains to the room-code COMMITMENT (the host pubkey's fingerprint MUST equal the room code) so a successor can't forge under a different key; reused the buildAttestPayload injective length-prefixed encoding (no malleability) + WebCrypto raw P-1363 sigs. LESSON: for risky infra, separate 'capability exists + is tested' (D1) from 'capability is active in the live path' (D2+) — additive-optional fields with fail-closed parsers make the dormant phase truly inert.
